step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select low-lying clayey or loamy soil with good water retention (pH 5.5–7.0).
Deep plough 2–3 times using a disc plough to 20–25 cm depth to break hardpan.
Apply 10–15 tonnes/hectare of well-decomposed FYM (farmyard manure) or compost and mix into soil.
Flood the field and puddle using a rotavator or puddler 2–3 days before transplanting to create anaerobic conditions.
Level the field with a leveller to ensure uniform water distribution (height variation < 5 cm).
Construct bunds 20–25 cm high around the field to retain water.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Select certified seeds of recommended variety (e.g., IR-64, Swarna, MTU-7029 or local high-yield variety); seed rate: 25–30 kg/ha for transplanted, 80–100 kg/ha for direct seeded.
Perform salt water test: dissolve 1 kg salt in 10 litres water; float seeds, discard floaters, wash sinkers thoroughly.
Treat seeds with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed OR Trichoderma viride 4g/kg seed to prevent fungal diseases.
For bacterial blight, treat with Streptocycline 0.01% (1g/10 litres water) for 30 minutes before sowing.
Pre-soak seeds in water for 24 hours, then incubate in wet gunny bag for 24–48 hours until 2–3 mm sprout emerges.

step_3_nursery_preparation.title: Nursery Preparation
steps: Prepare nursery bed of 1000 sq.m per hectare of main field.
Apply 1 kg urea + 1 kg SSP per 10 sq.m of nursery bed.
Sow pre-germinated seeds uniformly at 50–60 g/sq.m.
Maintain 2–3 cm standing water in nursery for first 7 days.
Apply Carbofuran 3G @ 1g/sq.m at 5 days after sowing for stem borer control.
Seedlings are ready for transplanting at 21–25 days (3–4 leaf stage, height 20–25 cm).

step_4_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Uproot seedlings carefully, tie in bundles and trim leaf tips by 1/3 to reduce transpiration.
Transplant 2–3 seedlings per hill at 20×15 cm spacing (row × plant).
Maintain 2–3 cm standing water at time of transplanting.
Apply basal dose fertilizer before transplanting: 40 kg N + 60 kg P2O5 + 40 kg K2O per hectare.
Transplant during morning or evening hours to reduce transplanting shock.
Gap filling: replace dead/missing seedlings within 7 days of transplanting.

step_5_irrigation.title: Irrigation Management
steps: Maintain 5 cm standing water during tillering stage (Day 36–70).
Allow field to dry (crack) once at tillering for 2–3 days to stimulate root growth (alternate wet/dry).
Maintain 2–3 cm water at panicle initiation (Day 71–95) — critical period.
Maintain 3–5 cm water during flowering (Day 96–110) — do not allow field to dry.
Gradually reduce water during grain filling (Day 111–135): irrigate every 5–7 days.
Stop irrigation 10–15 days before harvest to allow field drying for easy harvesting.
Total water requirement: 1200 mm. Frequency: every 3–5 days in dry weather.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 87 kg/ha + SSP 375 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha (provides N:P:K = 40:60:40 kg/ha)
Zinc Sulphate 25 kg/ha as basal if Zn deficiency observed (yellowing of young leaves)

step_7_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Apply Butachlor 50 EC @ 3–4 litres/ha OR Pretilachlor 50 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha within 3 days of transplanting.
Post-emergence (Day 25–30 after transplanting): Apply Bispyribac-sodium 10 SC @ 200 ml/ha for mixed weeds.
Manual weeding at Day 20 and Day 40 after transplanting using a hand weeder or conoweeder.
Maintain standing water (5 cm) immediately after herbicide application for 3–4 days.

pests.symptoms: Dead heart in vegetative, white ear in reproductive stage
pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha OR Cartap Hydrochloride 4G @ 18 kg/ha at tillering

pests.symptoms: Hopper burn, yellowing and drying of plants in circular patches
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 125 ml/ha OR Buprofezin 25 SC @ 800 ml/ha

diseases.symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions with grey centre on leaves/neck
diseases.control: Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 300 g/ha OR Isoprothiolane 40 EC @ 750 ml/ha

diseases.symptoms: Water-soaked to yellow lesions from leaf tip/margin
diseases.control: Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha + Streptocycline 500 ppm

diseases.symptoms: Oval/irregular lesions on sheath near waterline
diseases.control: Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha OR Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha

step_10_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Stop irrigation 10–15 days before harvest.
Harvest when grain moisture is 20–25%; avoid delayed harvest to reduce shattering.
Cut crop 15–20 cm above ground using sickle or combine harvester.
Thresh immediately or within 24 hours using thresher @ 500–600 RPM.
Winnow to separate grain from chaff.
Expected yield: 4–6 tonnes/ha (irrigated), 2–3 tonnes/ha (rainfed).

step_11_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Sun-dry grain to 14% moisture content (takes 3–5 days).
Clean grain using seed cleaner/grader.
Store in gunny bags or metallic bins treated with Malathion 50 EC @ 3 ml/litre on bag surface.
Store in cool, dry, ventilated warehouse. Stack bags on wooden pallets, away from walls.
Apply Aluminium Phosphide tablet 1 tablet/tonne in sealed storage to prevent insect infestation.
Straw management: Incorporate stubble into soil by deep ploughing or use as mulch/fodder.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil with pH 6.0–7.5.
Deep plough to 25–30 cm with a mould-board or disc plough.
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM or compost and incorporate with 2nd ploughing.
Harrowing 2–3 times to achieve fine tilth.
Form ridges and furrows at 60–75 cm row spacing for improved drainage.
Ensure no waterlogging — maize is highly sensitive to standing water.

step_2_seed_selection_and_treatment.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
steps: Use certified hybrid seed (e.g., DKC 9144, Pioneer 30V92, Dekalb 9108); seed rate: 20–25 kg/ha.
Treat seed with Thiram 75 WS @ 2.5 g/kg seed OR Captan 75 WP @ 2.5 g/kg seed.
For stalk rot, treat with Metalaxyl 35 WS @ 2 g/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azospirillum 600 g/ha + PSB (Phosphate Solubilizing Bacteria) 600 g/ha mixed in 50 ml water per kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow 2–3 seeds per hill at 5–7 cm depth.
Spacing: 60–75 cm between rows × 20–25 cm between plants.
Apply basal fertilizer in furrow before sowing: N:P:K = 60:80:40 kg/ha.
Cover seeds with soil and firm with foot for proper seed-soil contact.
Thinning: Remove extra seedlings leaving 1 plant per hill at Day 10–12.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation Management
steps: Apply pre-sowing irrigation if soil is dry.
Irrigate at 10–12 day intervals during vegetative stage.
Critical irrigation stages: Tasseling (Day 56–70) and Silking (Day 71–80) — do not stress.
Grain filling (Day 81–100): irrigate every 10 days.
Total 5–6 irrigations required; total water 500 mm.
Avoid waterlogging at all stages.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 174 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha (N:P:K = 60:80:40 kg/ha)
Urea 130 kg/ha (60 kg N/ha); apply in furrows beside plants, cover with soil
Zinc Sulphate 25 kg/ha basal; Boron 1 kg/ha foliar spray at tasseling

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Atrazine 50 WP @ 1.5–2 kg/ha within 2–3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence (Day 20–25): Tembotrione 34.4 SC @ 120 ml/ha for broadleaf and grassy weeds.
Manual inter-cultivation at Day 20 and Day 40 using hand hoe or tractor-mounted cultivator.
Earth up plants at Day 30–35 to support stems and cover surface roots.

pests.symptoms: Window pane feeding, sawdust-like frass in whorl
pests.control: Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 425 g/ha OR Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha; apply into whorl
Imidacloprid 17.8 SL seed treatment 5 ml/kg seed; foliar Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha

pests.symptoms: Dead heart, bore holes in stem
pests.control: Carbofuran 3G @ 15 kg/ha in whorl at Day 15–20

diseases.symptoms: Long, elliptical, tan lesions on leaves
diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha; spray at 10-day intervals
Metalaxyl 35 WS seed treatment 2 g/kg; foliar Mancozeb 0.25% spray
Avoid waterlogging; apply Potash adequately; seed treatment with Thiram

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_9_harvesting.timing: Day 101–110 (when husk turns brown, black layer formed at kernel base)
steps: Harvest cobs when grain moisture is 25–30%; dry before threshing.
Remove cobs by hand or use maize harvester.
Husk cobs and sun-dry for 5–7 days to reach 12–14% moisture.
Shell using maize sheller at 500 RPM.
Expected yield: 6–10 tonnes/ha (hybrid), 3–4 tonnes/ha (open-pollinated).

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest Management
steps: Dry grain to 12–14% moisture before storage.
Grade to remove broken kernels and foreign matter.
Store in moisture-proof metallic bins or gunny bags on pallets.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide 1 tablet/tonne in sealed storage.
Stover can be used as silage, fodder, or incorporated as green manure.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select medium to deep black cotton soil or red loamy soil with pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough once to 20–25 cm; cross harrow 2 times for fine tilth.
Apply 8–10 tonnes/ha FYM and mix during final ploughing.
Form ridges and furrows at 45 cm spacing if rainfall is expected to be heavy.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 10–12 kg/ha for hybrids; 12–15 kg/ha for varieties.
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed + Imidacloprid 70 WS @ 7 ml/kg seed.
Dry treat seeds 24 hours before sowing.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow on ridges or flat beds at 2–3 cm depth.
Row spacing: 45 cm; plant spacing: 15 cm (thin to 1 plant/hill at Day 10).
Apply basal: DAP 100 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha at sowing.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Sorghum is drought tolerant but critical irrigation at booting (Day 61–75) and flowering (Day 76–85).
Irrigate every 12–15 days in absence of rain.
Total 3–4 irrigations in case of deficit rainfall.

schedule.fertilizers: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha; Apply as DAP + MOP + Urea

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Atrazine 50 WP @ 1 kg/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Hand weeding at Day 20 and Day 40.
Watch for Striga (witchweed): use resistant varieties + Ethylene-based stimulants for suicidal germination.

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha at whorl stage

diseases.control: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha at heading
Avoid moisture stress at grain filling; apply Potash adequately
Metalaxyl seed treatment; spray Mancozeb 0.25% at 10-day intervals

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_8_harvesting.timing: Day 106–110, when grain is hard and moisture < 25%
steps: Cut panicles with sickle; sun-dry for 5–7 days.
Thresh with thresher or by beating panicles.
Expected yield: 3–5 tonnes/ha (hybrid grain sorghum).

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry grain to 12% moisture before storage.
Store in bins with Aluminium Phosphide fumigation.
Stover used as valuable cattle fodder; stack in shade.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select sandy loam to loamy soil; tolerates poor soil (pH 6.0–8.0).
Plough once to 20 cm, harrow 2 times.
Apply 5–8 tonnes/ha FYM or compost as basal.
Avoid waterlogged or heavy clay soils.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 5 kg/ha for hybrids; 8–10 kg/ha for varieties.
Treat with Apron 35 SD (Metalaxyl) @ 6 g/kg seed for downy mildew.
Bio-treat with Trichoderma viride 4g/kg seed.
Inoculate with Azospirillum 600 g/ha.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 3–4 cm depth; row spacing 45 cm × plant spacing 10–15 cm.
After germination, thin to 1 plant/hill at Day 10–12.
Basal fertilizer: N:P:K = 40:20:20 kg/ha at sowing.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg + SSP 125 kg + MOP 33 kg per ha

step_5_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Mostly rainfed; if deficit, critical irrigation at flowering (Day 51–65).
2–3 supplemental irrigations of 40–50 mm each if needed.

pests.control: Carbofuran 3G @ 10 kg/ha in whorl at Day 15–20
Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 4 litres/ha soil drench at sowing

diseases.control: Metalaxyl 35 WS @ 6 g/kg seed treatment; Mancozeb spray 0.25%
Infected panicles: spray Copper Oxychloride 0.25% at flowering
Carbendazim 50 WP @ 2g/kg seed treatment

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Cut panicles using sickle; thresh by beating.
Sun dry to 12% moisture.
Expected yield: 2–4 tonnes/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Clean, dry (10–12% MC) and store in metal bins.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide.
Stover used as fodder — stack under shade.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select red laterite or well-drained loamy soil (pH 5.5–7.0).
Deep plough once; cross-harrow 2 times.
Apply 8–10 tonnes/ha compost or FYM.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 10 kg/ha (direct seeding); 2.5 kg/ha (transplanting).
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed + Thiram 2g/kg seed (combined).
Inoculate with Azospirillum 200 g/ha.

step_3_nursery_and_transplanting.title: Nursery & Transplanting
steps: Prepare nursery; sow seeds at 5 g/sq.m.
Transplant at 25–30 days at 22.5×10 cm spacing.
Or direct seed at 30×10 cm spacing; thin to 1 plant/hill.

step_5_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Mainly rainfed; critical irrigation at flowering (Day 61–80).
3–4 irrigations of 40 mm each if rains are deficient.

pests.control: Endosulfan 35 EC @ 1 litre/ha at milky stage

diseases.control: Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 500 g/ha; spray at boot leaf stage
Seed treatment with Captan 75 WP @ 2 g/kg

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 116–120 when earheads dry and seeds harden
steps: Cut earheads with sickle; thresh by rolling over dry surface.
Winnow; sun-dry to 12% moisture.
Expected yield: 2–3 tonnes/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store in clean, dry, ventilated granaries.
Ragi stores well due to presence of tannins — insect-resistant.
Straw used as nutritious fodder.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select deep, well-drained loamy or black cotton soil (pH 6.0–7.5).
Deep plough to 30 cm once; harrow 2 times.
Apply 5–8 tonnes/ha FYM at last ploughing.
Form furrows at 60–90 cm row spacing.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 15–20 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed + Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1g/kg.
Rhizobium inoculation: 200 g Rhizobium (strain IC-3246) per 10 kg seed in 300 ml jaggery solution; shade-dry for 30 minutes.
PSB inoculation: 200 g/10 kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 5–7 cm depth; 90 cm × 20 cm spacing (sole crop).
For intercrop with Sorghum: 2:1 or 3:1 rows.
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha (Rhizobium fixes N, so minimal N needed).

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Mainly rainfed; critical periods: flowering (Day 61–100) and pod formation (Day 101–140).
If deficit: irrigate at flowering — 1–2 irrigations of 50 mm.
Avoid waterlogging — very sensitive.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha (P2O5 40 kg + K2O 20 kg/ha)
Urea 22 kg/ha at sowing (10 kg N/ha starter dose)
Zinc Sulphate 20 kg/ha if Zn deficiency; Boron 1 kg/ha foliar at flowering

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Hand weeding at Day 20–25 and Day 45–50.
Inter-cultivation with hand hoe or bullock-drawn cultivator at 3-week intervals.

pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha OR Indoxacarb 14.5 SC @ 500 ml/ha at pod initiation
Spray Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha at pod development

diseases.control: Use resistant variety; Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1g/kg seed treatment; Trichoderma soil application
Control eriophyid mite vector with Dicofol 18.5 EC @ 2 ml/litre
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre spray

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_8_harvesting.timing: Day 176–180 when pods turn dry and brown
steps: Harvest when 80% pods are mature.
Cut plants 15 cm above ground; stack for 5–7 days.
Thresh using thresher or by beating on hard surface.
Winnow; dry grain to 10–12% moisture.
Expected yield: 1–2 tonnes/ha.

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Sun-dry grain to 10% moisture before storage.
Mix with Neem leaf powder or store with Aluminium Phosphide tablet.
Straw incorporated in soil or used as fodder.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Sandy loam or well-drained loam; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once to 15–20 cm; 2 harrowings for fine tilth.
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM at final ploughing.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 20–25 kg/ha.
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed.
Rhizobium (strain TAL 169) inoculation: 200 g/10 kg seed.
PSB inoculation: 200 g/10 kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 4–5 cm depth; 30–45 cm × 10 cm spacing.
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha (SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha).
Starter N: Urea 22 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Critical irrigation at flowering (Day 26–45) and pod filling (Day 46–65).
2–3 irrigations of 40 mm each; avoid waterlogging.

schedule.fertilizers: 2% DAP solution (20 g DAP dissolved in 1 litre water)

pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha; use virus-resistant varieties
Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 1 litre/ha at pod formation

diseases.control: Karathane (Dinocap) 0.05% OR Sulphur dust 25 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha at 10-day intervals
Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride 4g/kg

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 66–75; pick at 80% pod maturity (pods dark brown)
steps: 2–3 pickings at 3–5 day intervals (pods mature unevenly).
Final harvest: cut plants, dry, thresh.
Expected yield: 1–1.5 tonnes/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10–11% moisture; store with Edible grade Neem oil 5 ml/kg grain.
Store in airtight containers or hermetic bags.
Shelf life: 6–8 months under proper storage.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained loamy to clay loam soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once; 2 harrowings.
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM or compost.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 20–25 kg/ha.
Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed.
Rhizobium (strain CB756) 200 g/10 kg seed in jaggery solution.
PSB 200 g/10 kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Depth 4–5 cm; spacing 30 cm × 10 cm.
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha; starter N 20 kg/ha.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg + SSP 250 kg + MOP 33 kg per ha

pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 100 ml/ha for whitefly; remove infected plants

diseases.control: No direct control; remove infected plants; control aphid vector
Trichoderma viride 4g/kg seed treatment; avoid waterlogging
Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Pick blackened pods; avoid over-mature pods that shatter.
Final harvest: cut and thresh.
Expected yield: 0.8–1.5 tonnes/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10% MC; store in airtight metallic or plastic bins.
Add Neem leaf or Deltamethrin-treated gunny bags to prevent pulse weevil.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Sandy loam or loam soil; pH 6.0–7.5; tolerates slightly acidic soils.
Plough once to 15–20 cm; 2 harrowings.
Apply 4–5 tonnes/ha FYM.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 25–30 kg/ha (grain); 40–45 kg/ha (fodder).
Treat with Thiram 3g/kg + Carbendazim 1g/kg.
Rhizobium (strain CC1192) inoculation: 200 g/10 kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Depth 4–5 cm; spacing 45 cm × 15–20 cm (grain); 30 cm × 10 cm (fodder).
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 20 kg/ha.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 33 kg/ha + Urea 22 kg/ha

pests.control: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 100 ml/ha at pod set
Carbofuran 3G @ 15 kg/ha at sowing; or Trichoderma

diseases.control: Virus-free seed; control aphid vectors
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_6_harvesting.timing: Day 71–80; multiple pickings for green pod; single for dry grain
steps: For green pods: pick every 3–5 days.
For dry grain: harvest when pods turn brown; thresh.
Expected yield: 1–1.5 t/ha (grain); 25–30 t/ha green fodder.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry grain to 10–11% MC; store in metal bins with Aluminium Phosphide.
Green pods marketed fresh; or blanched and dried.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Shallow, poor soils tolerated; prefers light to medium well-drained soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Minimum tillage acceptable; plough once, harrow once.
Apply 3–4 tonnes/ha FYM.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 25–30 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 2g/kg seed.
Rhizobium inoculation recommended.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Depth 4–5 cm; spacing 30 cm × 10 cm.
Minimal basal: P2O5 20 kg/ha + K2O 10 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Highly drought tolerant; grown mostly on residual moisture.
1 irrigation at flowering if dry conditions.

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 1 litre/ha at pod formation

diseases.control: Sulphur dust 25 kg/ha
Trichoderma viride seed treatment 4g/kg

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_6_harvesting.timing: Day 111–120 when pods brown and dry
steps: Cut plants; dry in sun; thresh by beating.
Expected yield: 0.6–1.0 t/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 10% MC; store with Neem leaf.
Used as cattle feed; green plants used as green manure.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select medium black or red loamy soil; pH 6.0–7.5.
Deep plough to 25 cm; cross-harrow 2 times.
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate with ploughing.
Ensure good drainage; form raised beds if heavy rainfall expected.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 80–100 kg/ha (depending on seed size).
Carbendazim 2g/kg + Thiram 2g/kg seed treatment (combined).
Rhizobium (strain CB1809) 5 g/kg seed in 10% sugar solution.
Molybdenum (sodium molybdate) 1g/kg seed — improves N fixation.
Do NOT use chemical fungicide along with Rhizobium — apply separately.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 3–4 cm depth; spacing 45 cm × 5 cm.
Seed-cum-fertilizer drill recommended.
Basal: P2O5 60 kg/ha + K2O 40 kg/ha + Starter N 20 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Critical at flowering (Day 41–65) and pod filling (Day 66–90).
Avoid drought stress during R1–R6 growth stages.
3–4 supplemental irrigations of 50 mm each if rainfall deficient.
Avoid waterlogging.

schedule.fertilizers: DAP 130 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha + Urea 43 kg/ha (N:P:K = 20:60:40)
Borax 0.2% spray at flowering to improve pod set

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha within 3 days of sowing.
Post-emergence: Imazethapyr 10 SL @ 1 litre/ha at Day 20–25.
Hand weeding at Day 20 and Day 40.

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha; remove wilted shoots
SLNPV 250 LE/ha OR Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha

diseases.control: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha
Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha
Control whitefly with Imidacloprid; remove infected plants

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_8_harvesting.timing: Day 106–110 when 90% pods turn brown and leaves shed
steps: Harvest at 15–20% grain moisture to prevent shattering.
Cut with sickle or combine; dry in swaths for 3–5 days.
Thresh using thresher at 500–600 RPM.
Expected yield: 2–3 tonnes/ha.

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 12% MC; soybean is more susceptible to mold than other pulses.
Store in cool, dry, ventilated godowns.
Fumigate with Aluminium Phosphide 1 tablet/tonne.
Straw (stubble) incorporated in soil improves fertility.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Select well-drained sandy loam or red loamy soil; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough to 25 cm; cross harrow 2–3 times to achieve fine, loose tilth.
Apply 5–8 tonnes/ha FYM or compost; incorporate gypsum 500 kg/ha as basal for calcium.
Avoid compacted soils — pegs cannot penetrate to form pods.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 100–120 kg/ha (bold varieties); 80–100 kg/ha (small varieties); shell pods manually.
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed OR Metalaxyl 35 WS @ 2g/kg.
Rhizobium (NC-92) 200 g/10 kg seed + PSB 200 g/10 kg seed.
Do not expose treated seed to direct sun; sow same day as treatment.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 5–6 cm depth; spacing 30 cm × 10 cm (bunch type) or 45 cm × 15 cm (spreading type).
Basal: P2O5 40 kg/ha + K2O 40 kg/ha + Ca (gypsum 500 kg/ha at peg formation).
Starter N: Urea 22 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Critical at flowering and pegging (Day 41–75) — do not allow drought.
Pod development (Day 76–105): irrigate every 10–12 days.
Apply 500 kg/ha gypsum around plants at pegging stage (Day 40–45) for Ca to developing pods.
Total 4–6 irrigations; stop 2 weeks before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 250 kg/ha + MOP 67 kg/ha + Urea 22 kg/ha
Gypsum 500 kg/ha as top dressing around plants

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha OR Metolachlor 50 EC @ 2 litres/ha.
Post-emergence (Day 25): Quizalofop-ethyl 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha for grassy weeds.
2 manual weedings at Day 20 and Day 35; light inter-cultivation avoids peg damage.

pests.control: Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha; or Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha
Phorate 10G @ 10 kg/ha or Chlorpyrifos 20 EC soil drench

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha at 10-day intervals from Day 30
Propiconazole 25 EC @ 500 ml/ha
Seed treatment with Trichoderma viride 4g/kg; drench Carbendazim at collar

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_8_harvesting.timing: Day 106–120 when leaves yellow and inner pod wall darkens
steps: Irrigate 2 days before harvest for easy digging.
Lift plants using cultivator or plough; shake off soil.
Stack plants in small heaps; sun-dry for 3–5 days.
Strip pods from plants manually or using pod stripper.
Expected yield: 2.5–4 tonnes/ha (in-shell pods).

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry pods to 8–10% moisture before storage.
Store in wicker baskets, gunny bags or bins in airy, cool store.
Protect from Aspergillus flavus (aflatoxin) — ensure thorough drying, avoid cracked pods.
For oil use: send to expeller; oil yield ~48–50% of kernel weight.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained sandy loam or red soil; pH 5.5–8.0.
Plough once to 20 cm; 2 harrowings for very fine tilth (tiny seeds need firm, fine seedbed).
Apply 5 tonnes/ha FYM at final ploughing.
Ridge and furrow system preferred (30–45 cm rows).

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 5 kg/ha; mix seed with dry sand 1:4 ratio for uniform sowing.
Treat with Agrosan GN @ 2g/kg seed OR Thiram 3g/kg.
Seed germination test: germination > 70% required.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Broadcast or sow in rows at 2–3 cm depth; row spacing 30–45 cm.
Mix seed with 4× volume dry sand before broadcasting for even distribution.
Thin to 10–15 cm plant spacing at Day 10–12.
Basal: N:P:K = 20:20:10 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: 2–3 critical irrigations: pre-sowing, at flowering (Day 36–60), capsule formation.
Avoid waterlogging — very sensitive to root rot.
Each irrigation 40–50 mm.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg + SSP 125 kg + MOP 17 kg per ha

pests.control: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1 litre/ha; destroy galled capsules

diseases.control: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl 35 WS 2g/kg; drainage improvement
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha; 2–3 sprays
Wettable sulphur 0.2%

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 86–90 when lower capsules begin to yellow-brown (before shattering)
steps: Cut plants at base with sickle early morning (less shattering).
Bundle plants and stand upright on tarpaulin; shake daily.
Thresh seeds after 5–7 days of sun drying.
Winnow and dry to 6–8% moisture.
Expected yield: 0.5–0.8 t/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 6% MC before storage (high oil content, prone to rancidity).
Store in airtight containers in cool, dark place.
Oil extraction by cold pressing preferred for quality.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Deep, well-drained sandy loam to medium black soil; pH 5.0–7.0.
Deep plough to 30–35 cm (deep-rooted crop).
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate with final ploughing.
Raised beds (1 m wide) with furrows recommended for drainage.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 8–10 kg/ha (hybrids); 12–15 kg/ha (varieties).
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 4g/kg seed.
For wilt: soak seeds in Carbendazim 50 WP 0.1% solution for 30 min.
No Rhizobium needed; castor is non-legume.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 5 cm depth; spacing 90 cm × 60 cm (hybrid) or 60 cm × 45 cm (variety).
2 seeds per hill; thin to 1 plant at Day 10–12.
Basal: N:P:K = 40:40:20 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: 3–4 irrigations; critical at flowering (Day 61–100).
Irrigate every 15 days during capsule development.
Stop irrigation 3 weeks before harvest.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 87 kg + DAP 87 kg + MOP 33 kg per ha

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha; collect and destroy capsules
Neem oil 0.3% OR Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 100 ml/ha

diseases.control: Trichoderma viride 2.5 kg/ha soil application; Carbendazim seed treatment
Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Iprodione 50 WP @ 1 kg/ha

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Every 25–30 days per spike; 5–6 harvests total over 180 days
steps: Harvest each spike when 50–60% capsules turn red/brown.
Pick spikes and sun-dry for 5–7 days; capsules split and shed seeds.
Collect seeds; winnow; remove remaining hulls.
Expected yield: 2–3 t/ha (hybrid); 1–1.5 t/ha (variety).

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry seeds to 7–8% MC.
Store in gunny bags in cool, dry store.
Castor oil content: 48–50% of kernel; extracted by cold/hot pressing.
WARNING: Ricin present in seed cake — do not use cake as food/fodder without detoxification.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Red, laterite or medium black soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Plough once, harrow 2 times.
Apply 4–5 tonnes/ha FYM; ridge-furrow system preferred.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 10–12 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 2g/kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Broadcast or row sowing; row spacing 30–45 cm.
Thin to 10 cm plant spacing at Day 15.
Basal: N:P:K = 20:20:10 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Mainly rainfed; 1–2 critical irrigations at flowering (Day 51–80) if deficit.

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Cut plants in early morning; stack for 5 days.
Thresh; winnow.
Expected yield: 0.5–0.8 t/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry to 6–8% MC.
Store in airtight containers.
Oil content 35–40%.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Deep black cotton soil (Vertisol) or red loam; pH 6.0–8.0.
Deep plough to 35–40 cm (cotton has deep taproot).
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM at last ploughing.
Subsoiling once in 3 years to break hardpan.
Form ridges and furrows at 90–120 cm spacing for drainage.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 1.5–2 kg/ha (Bt hybrid — delinted); 10–12 kg/ha (desi cotton).
Bt cotton: already treated by company; do not rewash.
Non-Bt: Treat with Imidacloprid 70 WS @ 5g/kg + Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed.
Bio-inoculate with Azospirillum 600 g/ha + PSB 600 g/ha.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
step_3_sowing.timing: June (at onset of monsoon, after 50–60 mm rain)
steps: Depth: 3–5 cm; spacing 90–120 cm × 45–60 cm (Bt hybrid).
Dibble planting — 2 seeds/hole; thin to 1 plant after germination.
Basal: P2O5 60 kg/ha + K2O 60 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Pre-sowing irrigation if soil is dry.
Critical at squaring (Day 71–95) and flowering (Day 96–120): every 8–10 days.
Boll development (Day 121–160): every 10–15 days.
Total 6–8 irrigations; stop 3 weeks before final harvest.
Drip irrigation at 60% ETc reduces water use by 40%.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg/ha + MOP 100 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha (P:K:starter N)
Borax 0.2% at flowering and boll setting
KNO3 1% at boll development for boll retention

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Diuron 80 WP @ 1.25 kg/ha OR Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 litres/ha.
Post-emergence: Quizalofop-ethyl 5 EC @ 1 litre/ha for grassy weeds.
Inter-cultivation at Day 20, 40, and 60 with hand hoe or tractor cultivator.
Avoid deep inter-cultivation near plant (root damage).

pests.control: Bt cotton is resistant to Helicoverpa. For pink/spotted: Indoxacarb 14.5 SC @ 500 ml/ha OR Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 150 ml/ha
Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 150 ml/ha (whitefly); Spiromesifen 22.9 SC @ 750 ml/ha (mites)
Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha OR Fipronil 5 SC @ 600 ml/ha
Profenofos 50 EC @ 1.5 litres/ha; release Cryptolaemus biocontrol agent

diseases.control: Copper Oxychloride 50 WP @ 2.5 kg/ha; Streptocycline 100 ppm
Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP drench at collar
Control whitefly vector; use resistant varieties

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_9_harvesting.timing: Day 161–180 and beyond; 3–4 pickings over season
steps: First picking: when 50–60% bolls open (normally 130–150 DAE).
Pick in early morning; collect in clean cloth bags (not gunny).
Pick every 15–20 days thereafter.
Avoid rain-wetted or dew-covered cotton.
Expected yield: 2–3 t/ha seed cotton (hybrid).

step_10_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Sun-dry picked cotton for 2–3 days to reduce moisture.
Sort and remove trash, leaves, and immature bolls.
Send to ginning factory; ginning outturn: 35–40% lint from seed cotton.
Cotton seeds sent for oil extraction (20% oil); oil cake used as cattle feed.
Deep-plough field after crop to destroy residues.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Alluvial loamy soil (Gangetic plain preferred); pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once to 25 cm; 3–4 harrowings for fine tilth (small seed).
Apply 4–5 tonnes/ha compost.
Raised beds 1.5 m wide with drainage channels (jute likes moisture but not waterlogging).

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 7–8 kg/ha.
Soak seeds in water for 12 hours before sowing for better germination.
Treat with Thiram 75 WS @ 3g/kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Broadcast or sow in rows 25–30 cm apart at 2–3 cm depth.
Thin to 4–5 cm plant spacing at Day 20–25.
Basal: N:P:K = 40:20:20 kg/ha; apply in bands.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: If monsoon-grown: no irrigation needed after establishment.
Pre-sowing irrigation if soil is dry.
3–4 irrigations for summer-sown crop.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 125 kg + MOP 33 kg per ha

diseases.control: Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1g/litre soil drench at base
Use clean water for retting; complete process within 20 days
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha spray

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 111–120 at initial flower bud stage (50% flowering) for best fibre quality
steps: Cut plants at base; remove leaves.
Bundle and immerse in pond/stream for retting (bacterial decomposition) for 14–20 days.
Check retting completion: fibre peels easily from stick.
Strip fibre; wash thoroughly with clean water.
Sun-dry fibre for 2–3 days.
Expected yield: 2.5–3.5 t/ha fibre.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Dry fibre to 12–14% MC; bundle in bales.
Grade fibre by colour, strength, and uniformity.
Sell to jute mills; top grades used for hessian cloth.
Jute sticks used as fuel or for making lightweight boards.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Deep, well-drained loamy soil or black cotton soil; pH 6.5–7.5.
Deep plough to 35–40 cm; subsoil if hardpan is present.
Apply 25 tonnes/ha FYM + 2 tonnes/ha compost.
Form furrows at 75–90 cm spacing for sett planting.
Incorporate lime if pH < 6.0 at 2 t/ha.

step_2_sett_preparation.title: Seed Sett Preparation
steps: Select disease-free, healthy 8–10 month old canes from certified seed nursery.
Cut 2–3 budded setts (25–30 cm long) using sharp, disinfected blade.
Treat setts with Aretan 6 (Methyl Mercury) 0.25% OR Bavistin 0.1% solution for 5 minutes — immerse and shade-dry.
Alternatively, Hot Water Treatment: 50°C for 2 hours — controls ratoon stunting disease.
Sett rate: 35,000–40,000 setts/ha (3-budded, 30 cm gap).

step_3_planting.title: Planting
steps: Place setts end-to-end in furrows at 5–8 cm depth.
Spacing: 75–90 cm between rows; setts touching end-to-end in row.
Cover with 5 cm soil; firm with foot.
Basal: N:P:K = 80:80:80 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Immediately after planting: light irrigation to settle soil around setts.
Germination (Day 0–30): irrigate every 5–7 days (70–80% SMC).
Tillering (Day 31–120): every 7–10 days.
Grand Growth (Day 121–270): every 10–15 days.
Maturity (Day 271–365): reduce to every 15–20 days.
Total: 20–25 irrigations; each 60–80 mm.
Drip irrigation at 80% ETc saves 30–40% water.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 500 kg/ha + MOP 133 kg/ha + Urea 87 kg/ha
Urea 174 kg/ha (80 kg N) + MOP 67 kg/ha (K2O 40 kg)
ZnSO4 25 kg/ha + Silica 100 kg/ha at planting

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence (Day 0–3 after planting): Atrazine 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha.
Post-emergence (Day 30): 2,4-D 80 WP @ 1.25 kg/ha for broadleaf weeds.
Manual weeding at Day 30, 60, and 90.
Earthing up at Day 90–120 (suppresses weeds, supports stalks).
Trash mulching (after 1st year) suppresses subsequent weeds.

pests.control: Carbofuran 3G @ 33 kg/ha at Day 30–45; or drench Chlorpyrifos 20 EC
Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha foliar at Day 90–120
Quinalphos 25 EC @ 2 litres/ha; IISR biocontrol agents (Epiricania)

diseases.control: Hot water sett treatment; fungicide Carbendazim 0.1% sett dip; use resistant variety
Hot water treatment; rogue out infected plants (whip stage)
Use HWT (50°C, 2 hrs); use disease-free planting material
Use resistant variety; Carbendazim 0.1% sett treatment

step_9_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_9_harvesting.timing: 10–12 months after planting (January–March for max sucrose)
steps: Check brix > 18% and pol% > 14% using refractometer before harvest.
De-trash (remove dry leaves) 2–3 months before harvest.
Stop irrigation 3–4 weeks before harvest.
Cut canes at ground level; remove tops and leaves.
Transport to mill within 24 hours (sucrose reduces with time).
Expected yield: 80–120 tonnes/ha cane.

step_10_ratoon_management.title: Ratoon Crop Management
steps: After harvest, cut stubs flush with ground using sharp blade.
Apply 25 tonnes/ha FYM around stubs.
Gap filling: plant fresh setts in gaps where germination is poor.
Apply full fertilizer dose same as plant crop.
Ratoon yield: 60–80% of plant crop; maximum 3 ratoons recommended.

step_11_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Trash (dry leaves) used for mulching next crop.
Press mud (mill byproduct) returned to field as manure.
Bagasse used for co-generation in mill.
Molasses used for alcohol/ethanol production.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Light sandy loam to medium loam soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Deep plough to 30 cm; 3–4 harrowings for very fine tilth.
Apply 10 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate with ploughing.
Raised beds 1.2 m wide recommended for drainage.

step_2_nursery.title: Nursery Preparation
steps: Prepare raised nursery beds of 1 m × 10 m.
Sterilize soil with Formalin 2% solution 15 days before sowing; cover with polythene.
Sow seeds very thinly: 6–8 g/100 sq.m; cover with fine soil.
Spray Captan 0.2% after sowing.
Seedlings ready at 45–55 days (height 15–20 cm, 4–5 leaves).

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Transplant in cool hours (morning or evening).
Spacing: 90 cm × 60 cm; 90 cm × 45 cm (flue-cured type).
Apply basal fertilizer in furrows: N:P:K = 80:60:80 kg/ha.
Drench transplanted seedlings with Carbendazim 0.1% for root protection.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: At transplanting: light irrigation immediately.
Vegetative (Day 61–100): every 7–10 days; 40–50 mm each.
Maturity (Day 101–125): reduce water.
Stop 2 weeks before harvest.

step_5_fertilizer.title: Fertilizer

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg/ha + MOP 133 kg/ha + Urea 87 kg/ha (split N)

step_5_fertilizer.note: Do NOT over-apply N — reduces leaf quality and burning characteristics

step_6_cultural_practices.title: Special Cultural Practices
steps: Topping (Day 70–75): Remove growing apex when 50% plants start flowering; allows leaf expansion.
Desuckering (weekly after topping): Remove suckers arising from leaf axils; apply MH-30 (Maleic Hydrazide 0.35%) to retard sucker growth.
Remove lowest 2–3 ground leaves (sand leaves) as they reduce quality.

pests.control: SLNPV 250 LE/ha OR Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha

diseases.control: Soil drenching with Thiram 0.2%
Wash hands with soap before handling; remove infected plants; use TMV-tolerant variety
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre

step_8_harvesting_and_curing.title: Harvesting & Curing
step_8_harvesting_and_curing.timing: Day 126–130; leaves harvested bottom to top over 8–10 weeks
steps: Harvest leaves at 75–80% maturity (light green to yellow-green colour).
Pick 2–3 leaves per plant per week from bottom upwards.
Flue-cured tobacco: string leaves on sticks; cure in barn at 35°C → 70°C over 5–7 days.
Air-cured (burley): hang in open barn for 30–45 days.
Sun-cured: spread on tarpaulin; 2–3 weeks sun drying.
Expected yield: 1.5–2.5 t/ha cured leaf.

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Sort/grade leaves by size, colour, and texture.
Bale into 50–60 kg bales; press using tobacco press.
Store in humid warehouse (65% RH) to prevent brittleness.
Market through Tobacco Board auctions.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to loam; pH 6.0–7.5.
Plough once to 20 cm; 2 harrowings.
Apply 20–25 tonnes/ha FYM; incorporate with last ploughing.

step_2_seed_treatment.title: Seed Treatment
steps: Seed rate: 10–12 kg/ha.
Soak in water 8–10 hours to improve germination.
Treat with Carbendazim 2g/kg seed.

step_3_sowing.title: Sowing
steps: Sow at 2–3 cm depth; row spacing 45–60 cm × plant spacing 30 cm.
2 seeds/hill; thin to 1 plant at Day 10–12.
Basal: N:P:K = 50:50:50 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Weekly irrigation during fruiting (Day 41–90); 30–40 mm.
Critical at flowering and pod development.
Avoid standing water around stem.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 109 kg + DAP 109 kg + MOP 83 kg per ha

pests.pest: YVMV (Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus via whitefly)
pests.control: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 125 ml/ha; use YVMV-resistant variety (Ex: Arka Anamika)
Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 220 g/ha OR Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha
Abamectin 1.9 EC @ 500 ml/ha OR Spiromesifen 22.9 SC @ 750 ml/ha

diseases.control: Karathane 0.1% OR Trifloxystrobin 25% + Tebuconazole 50% WG 400 g/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Seed treatment Thiram 4g/kg; Captan drench at seedling stage

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest tender pods at 5–7 cm length; do not allow to over-mature (fibre development).
Pick with a sharp knife or snap cleanly to avoid plant damage.
Expected yield: 8–15 t/ha.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Pre-cool harvested pods within 2 hours of picking.
Store at 10–13°C, 90–95% RH; shelf life 7–10 days.
Grade by size and grade for market.
Process excess: dry sliced okra or pickle.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 5.5–7.0.
Plough 2–3 times; apply 25 t/ha FYM.
Beds of 1 m width for nursery; raised beds for field.

step_2_nursery.title: Nursery Raising
steps: Seed rate: 250–400 g/ha (very small seeds).
Sow on raised nursery beds; cover with fine sand.
Treat seeds with Thiram 4g/kg.
Spray Mancozeb 0.25% 1 week after germination.
Transplant at 5–6 leaf stage (Day 35–40).

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Spacing: 60 cm × 45 cm or 75 cm × 60 cm.
Drench transplanted seedlings with Carbendazim 0.1%.
Basal: N:P:K = 75:60:60 kg/ha.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg + MOP 100 kg + Urea 65 kg per ha

pests.control: Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 220 g/ha; remove and destroy bored shoots/fruits

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Copper Oxychloride 2.5 kg/ha alternately
No chemical control; rogue infected plants; control leafhopper vector with Imidacloprid
Avoid growing in infested soil; Trichoderma + FYM application

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_6_harvesting.timing: First harvest Day 60–70 after transplanting; every 5–7 days thereafter
steps: Harvest at immature, tender stage (skin glossy, flesh firm).
Cut fruit with stalk using sharp knife.
Expected yield: 25–40 t/ha.

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Sort and grade by size and colour.
Store at 12°C, 90–95% RH for 7–10 days.
Avoid chilling below 10°C (chilling injury).
Local market: sell within 24 hours of harvest.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Sandy loam to loam; well drained; pH 6.0–7.0.
Deep plough 25 cm; 2–3 harrowings.
Apply 25–30 t/ha FYM; form raised beds.

step_2_nursery.title: Nursery
steps: Seed rate: 300–400 g/ha.
Raise on protrays or raised beds; treat with Thiram 4g/kg.
Drench nursery with Mancozeb 0.25% after germination.
Transplant at 25–30 days (4–5 leaf stage).

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Spacing: 60 cm × 45 cm; use drip tape alongside row.
Apply Trichoderma viride 2.5 kg/ha mixed in FYM 50 kg around transplant hole.
Basal: N:P:K = 100:80:60 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Critical at transplanting, flowering, and fruit set.
Drip irrigation at 3–4 mm/day; or flood irrigation every 5–7 days.
Avoid water stress at flowering — causes blossom drop.
Avoid over-irrigation at ripening — causes fruit cracking.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 500 kg + MOP 100 kg + Urea 87 kg per ha
Urea 109 kg/ha (50 kg N) + KNO3 1% foliar
CaCl2 0.5% spray weekly to prevent blossom end rot

step_6_staking.title: Staking
steps: Drive bamboo stake 1.2–1.5 m tall next to each plant.
Tie plant to stake with soft twine at 30 cm intervals.
Pinch off suckers from indeterminate varieties for single/double stem.

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Chlorothalonil 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha alternately
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre; spray at 5-day intervals in cool, humid weather
Control whitefly; use TYLCV-resistant hybrids
Soil solarisation; Trichoderma application; avoid infected soil

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Mature green stage for distant market; breaker stage for local.
Harvest every 3–5 days.
Expected yield: 30–60 t/ha (hybrid); 20–30 t/ha (open-pollinated).

step_9_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Grade by size and colour (Grade A > 6 cm; B 4–6 cm).
Store at 12–15°C, 90% RH for 2–3 weeks.
Process: puree, ketchup, dried tomato slices.
Avoid stacking > 4 layers to prevent bruising.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
2–3 ploughings; apply 25 t/ha FYM.
Form raised beds.

step_2_nursery.title: Nursery
steps: Seed rate: 750 g–1 kg/ha.
Treat with Thiram 4g/kg; sow on raised beds.
Transplant at 30–35 days (5–6 leaves).

step_3_transplanting.title: Transplanting
steps: Spacing: 60 cm × 45 cm.
Drench with Carbendazim 0.1% after transplanting.
Basal: N:P:K = 75:75:50 kg/ha.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 469 kg + MOP 83 kg + Urea 65 kg per ha

pests.control: Spinosad 45 SC @ 73 ml/ha; reflective mulch to repel thrips

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha; spray before and after harvest
Rogue out; control vectors
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre soil drench

step_6_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_6_harvesting.timing: First harvest Day 75–90 after transplanting; multiple pickings
steps: Green chilli: harvest at full size but green.
Red chilli (dry): harvest when fully red; dry in sun for 10–15 days to 10% MC.
Expected yield: 20–30 t/ha (green); 3–5 t/ha (dry red).

step_7_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Green chilli: store at 5–8°C, 90% RH for 2–3 weeks.
Dry red chilli: stored at ambient in cool, dry store; pack in jute or cloth bags.
Processing: ground into chilli powder; oleoresin extraction for industry.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0; well drained.
Prepare pits 60×60×30 cm at 2.5 m × 2.5 m spacing; fill with FYM + top soil.
Seed rate: 4–5 kg/ha; sow 2–3 seeds per pit at 2–3 cm depth.
Thin to 1–2 plants per pit at Day 10.

step_2_training.title: Training
steps: Erect pandal/trellis of 1.5–2 m height with GI wire and bamboo poles.
Train vines onto trellis from Day 15–20.
Pinch tip of main stem to encourage lateral branching.

schedule.fertilizers: FYM 5 kg/pit + SSP 50 g/pit + MOP 30 g/pit

pests.control: Malathion bait (50 ml Malathion + 500 ml molasses + 450 ml water per trap); DDVP spray

diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Control aphid vector; rogue plants

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest tender fruits at soft skin stage (5–7 days after fruit set).
Do not allow over-maturity — fruits turn woody.
Expected yield: 30–50 t/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store at 10–15°C, 90% RH for up to 2 weeks.
Market fresh.
Mature dried fruits used as utensils.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam, well-drained, pH 6.0–7.0.
Pits 45×45×45 cm at 2 m × 1.5 m spacing; fill with FYM.
Seed rate: 5–6 kg/ha; nick seeds on flat side for better germination.
Soak 12 hours; sow 2 seeds per pit at 2–3 cm depth.

step_2_training.title: Training
steps: Train on bamboo stakes 1.5–2 m; or horizontal trellis.
Pinch main stem at Day 20–25 to stimulate lateral branches.

schedule.fertilizers: FYM 5 kg + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g

pests.control: DDVP 76 EC @ 1 ml/litre; protein bait traps

diseases.control: Wettable Sulphur @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb + Carbendazim combined spray

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest tender fruits at green, immature stage.
Do not allow yellow or red colouring — fruits become bitter.
Expected yield: 10–20 t/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store at 10–12°C, 90% RH for 2–3 weeks.
Process into dried chips or extract bitter gourd juice.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Pits 45×45×30 cm at 2.5 m × 1.5 m; fill with FYM.
Seed rate: 4–5 kg/ha; sow 2–3 seeds per pit at 2 cm depth.
Thin to 1–2 plants at Day 10.

step_2_training.title: Training
steps: Erect trellis or pandal at 1.5 m height.
Train vines from Day 15–20.

schedule.fertilizers: FYM 5 kg/pit + SSP 50 g + MOP 30 g

diseases.control: Sulphur 80 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Harvest at immature, tender stage; ridges clearly defined.
Over-mature fruits are fibrous.
Expected yield: 15–25 t/ha.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam to loam; pH 6.0–7.0.
Pits 60×60×45 cm at 3 m × 3 m spacing; fill with FYM.
Seed rate: 3–4 kg/ha; sow 3 seeds per pit; thin to 2 plants.
Depth 3–4 cm.

schedule.fertilizers: FYM 10 kg/pit + SSP 60 g + MOP 40 g

pests.control: Protein bait traps + DDVP 76 EC @ 1 ml/litre

diseases.control: Tridemorph 80 EC @ 1 litre/ha OR Sulphur 80 WP
Control aphid vectors; rogue plants

step_4_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_4_harvesting.timing: Day 90–120 when skin turns orange/yellow and stem dries
steps: For immature (green) vegetable use: harvest at Day 60–70.
For mature pumpkin: harvest with 5 cm stalk attached.
Expected yield: 20–35 t/ha.

step_5_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Cure in sun for 7–10 days to harden skin (improves storage).
Store in cool, dry, well-ventilated room; shelf life 3–6 months.
Process: pumpkin halwa, dried slices, soup.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam, well drained; pH 6.0–7.0.
River bed cultivation popular (silt loam).
Pits 60×60×45 cm at 3 m × 2 m spacing.
Fill with FYM 10 kg + SSP 60 g + MOP 40 g per pit.
Seed rate: 3–4 kg/ha; 3 seeds/pit at 2–3 cm depth; thin to 2 plants.
Seed treatment: Thiram 3g/kg.

step_2_training.title: Vine Management
steps: Train vines in desired direction (away from irrigation channel).
After fruit set, retain 2–3 fruits per vine; remove remaining to get large fruits.
Place dry straw under fruit to prevent soil contact (rot prevention).

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 54 kg/ha + KNO3 1% foliar for fruit quality
CaNO3 0.5% at fruit development to prevent blossom end rot

diseases.control: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha + Carbendazim 50 WP @ 500 g/ha
Trifloxystrobin 25% + Tebuconazole 50% WG @ 400 g/ha
Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP drench; avoid fruit touching soil

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Signs of maturity: dull sound on tapping; yellow ground spot; dry tendril near fruit.
Cut with 5 cm stalk using sharp knife.
Handle carefully — no drop bruising.
Expected yield: 30–50 t/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store at 10–15°C, 90% RH for 2–3 weeks.
Grade by weight: Extra Large > 8 kg; Large 6–8 kg; Medium 4–6 kg.
Transport in padded crates.

step_1_soil_and_sowing.title: Soil Prep & Sowing
steps: Sandy loam; pH 6.0–7.5; well drained.
Pits 60×60×30 cm at 2.5 m × 1.5 m; fill with FYM.
Seed rate: 2–3 kg/ha; 3 seeds/pit; thin to 2 plants.
Seed treatment: Thiram 3g/kg seed.

schedule.fertilizers: Urea 43 kg/ha + KNO3 0.5% foliar for sweetness

step_3_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Reduce irrigation at 2 weeks before harvest to improve sugar content.
Irrigate every 5–7 days during vegetative and fruit development.

diseases.control: Trifloxystrobin + Tebuconazole WG @ 400 g/ha
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha
Avoid soil contact; Mancozeb spray

step_5_harvesting.title: Harvesting
steps: Signs of maturity: sweet aroma; easy separation from vine (slip stage); yellow skin.
Harvest in cool morning hours.
Expected yield: 15–25 t/ha.

step_6_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Store at 5–10°C; shelf life 2–3 weeks.
Highly perishable — sell within 5–7 days at ambient.
Process: juice, jam, dried slices.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained sandy loam or red loamy soil; pH 5.5–7.0.
Deep plough to 30 cm; 3–4 harrowings.
Apply 25 tonnes/ha FYM + 2 tonnes/ha compost.
Form raised beds 1 m wide × 30 cm height with 50 cm channels.

step_2_rhizome_selection.title: Rhizome Selection & Treatment
steps: Select healthy, disease-free, well-matured mother rhizomes weighing 25–30 g.
Treat with Mancozeb 0.3% solution for 30 minutes + Quinalphos 0.075% for 30 minutes.
Shade-dry treated rhizomes for 30 minutes before planting.
Rhizome rate: 2,000–2,500 kg/ha.

step_3_planting.title: Planting
steps: Dibble rhizomes in shallow pits 5–7 cm deep; spacing 45 cm × 25 cm.
Mulch with green leaves (Gliricidia, banana leaves) @ 12–15 tonnes/ha immediately after planting.
Basal: N:P:K = 30:60:80 kg/ha at planting.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Heavy irrigation requirement: 1000 mm total.
Irrigate every 5–7 days in absence of rain.
Do not allow soil to dry — rhizome development suffers.
Drip irrigation at 3–4 litres/plant/day highly efficient.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg/ha + MOP 133 kg/ha + Urea 65 kg/ha
Apply 7–8 t/ha green leaf mulch + Urea 54 kg/ha
Manually mound soil around plants + light N dose

step_6_weed_management.title: Weed Management
steps: Pre-emergence: Oxyfluorfen 23.5 EC @ 0.5 litres/ha at Day 3.
Manual weeding at Day 40, 80, and 120 followed by earthing up.
Mulching significantly reduces weed pressure.

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha at Day 45 and 90; remove infested shoots

diseases.control: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl; field drenching Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha spray
Use disease-free planting material; soil solarisation; no chemical control available

step_8_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_8_harvesting.timing: Day 236–240 (7–9 months after planting) when leaves yellow and dry
steps: Irrigate 2 days before for easy digging.
Dig carefully with spade; avoid rhizome damage.
Separate fingers and mother rhizomes.
Expected yield: 20–25 t/ha fresh; 5–6 t/ha dry.
Store best quality as seed rhizomes for next season.

step_9_curing_and_processing.title: Curing & Processing
steps: Boiling/Curing: Boil rhizomes in water for 45–60 min until soft (froth stops emerging); this gelatinizes starch and improves colour.
Sun dry for 10–15 days on clean cemented floor, turning daily.
Polishing: Polish dried rhizomes in drum or manually to remove rough skin.
Grind to powder using stone/disk mill.
Oleoresin extraction for food industry.
Dried turmeric yield: 20–25% of fresh weight; curcumin content 2–5%.

step_1_soil_preparation.title: Soil Preparation
steps: Well-drained, rich sandy loam or red loam; pH 5.5–6.5.
Deep plough to 30 cm; 4–5 harrowings for fine, loose tilth.
Apply 25–30 t/ha FYM + Trichoderma viride 2.5 kg/ha mixed in FYM.
Form raised beds 1 m wide × 30 cm high with drainage furrows.

step_2_rhizome_treatment.title: Rhizome Treatment
steps: Select fresh, firm rhizomes (fingers) 20–30 g each; 1–2 growing buds.
Treat with Mancozeb 0.3% + Quinalphos 0.075% for 30 minutes.
Shade-dry for 4–6 hours.
Rhizome rate: 2,000–2,500 kg/ha.

step_3_planting.title: Planting
steps: Plant in furrows at 5–7 cm depth; spacing 30 cm × 20 cm.
Cover with 2.5 cm soil.
Mulch immediately with green leaves @ 10–12 t/ha (Gliricidia/banana).
Basal: N:P:K = 30:60:60 kg/ha.

step_4_irrigation.title: Irrigation
steps: Irrigate every 5–7 days (more frequent in summer).
Critical at rhizome development (Day 101–180).
Sprinkler or drip preferred; 900 mm total.
Mulching reduces irrigation frequency significantly.

schedule.fertilizers: SSP 375 kg + MOP 100 kg + Urea 65 kg per ha
Urea 65 kg/ha + apply 5 t/ha fresh mulch
Earth up around clumps; add compost 2 kg/m row

pests.control: Chlorpyrifos 20 EC @ 2 litres/ha; remove and destroy infested shoots
Quinalphos 0.075% dip before planting; drench around clumps
Carbofuran 3G @ 33 kg/ha at planting; or Trichoderma bio-treatment

diseases.control: Most serious disease; drain field; drench Metalaxyl + Mancozeb 72 WP @ 3 g/litre around clumps at onset
Use healthy seed; soil solarisation; Trichoderma soil application
Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2 kg/ha spray

step_7_harvesting.title: Harvesting
step_7_harvesting.timing: Day 206–210 (6–8 months) when leaves dry; for green ginger Day 150–180
steps: Dig rhizomes carefully with spade after soil irrigation.
Clean soil; separate young fingers and mother rhizomes.
Expected yield: 20–25 t/ha fresh rhizomes.
Store best quality as seed for next season.

step_8_post_harvest.title: Post-Harvest
steps: Fresh ginger: store at 10–12°C, 90% RH; shelf life 3–4 months.
Dried ginger: clean rhizomes; dry in sun 7–10 days; MC reduced from 80% to 10%.
Yield recovery: 20–25% of fresh weight as dry ginger.
Processing: ginger powder, ginger oil, oleoresin, ginger paste.
Seed rhizomes: store in pits covered with sand in a cool area.
